text: {"claims":[{"statement":"One large egg yolk contains 200 mg of cholesterol.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"just one large egg yolk has 200 mg of cholesterol—making it one of the richest sources of dietary cholesterol","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly states the amount of cholesterol in a large egg yolk, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"one large egg yolk","comparator":"","outcome":"cholesterol content","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"200 mg","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Eggs contain additional nutrients that may help lower the risk for heart disease.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"eggs also contain additional nutrients that may help lower the risk for heart disease","entailment":"The source explicitly says eggs have nutrients that could reduce heart‑disease risk, which underlies the claim.","population":"","intervention":"eggs (nutrients)","comparator":"","outcome":"risk for heart disease","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"An egg contains about 5 g of fat, mostly monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"the moderate amount of fat in an egg, about 5 grams, is mostly monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat","entailment":"The quoted passage gives the quantity of fat and its type, directly supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"egg (fat)","comparator":"","outcome":"fat composition","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"about 5 g","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Dietary cholesterol and blood cholesterol are only weakly related.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"it’s also crucial to distinguish between dietary cholesterol and cholesterol in the blood, which are only weakly related","entailment":"The sentence states the relationship between dietary and blood cholesterol, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"dietary cholesterol","comparator":"cholesterol in the blood","outcome":"strength of relationship","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"only weakly","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"The 2015 Dietary Guidelines for Americans removed the recommendation to limit dietary cholesterol to 300 mg per day.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"the Dietary Guidelines for Americans 2015 removed the prior recommendation to limit consumption of dietary cholesterol to 300 mg per day","entailment":"The quote reports the policy change, confirming the claim.","population":"","intervention":"limit consumption of dietary cholesterol to 300 mg per day","comparator":"","outcome":"policy recommendation","timeframe":"2015","quantifiers":"300 mg per day","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Consuming up to one egg per day is not associated with increased heart disease risk in healthy individuals.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Research on moderate egg consumption in two large prospective cohort studies (nearly 40,000 men and over 80,000 women) found that up to one egg per day is not associated with increased heart disease risk in healthy individuals","entailment":"The quoted research finding directly states the lack of association between up to one egg daily and heart disease risk among healthy people.","population":"healthy men and women (≈120,000)","intervention":"up to one egg per day","comparator":"","outcome":"heart disease risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"up to one egg per day","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Food cholesterol has a smaller effect on blood total and LDL cholesterol than the mix of dietary fats.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"cholesterol in food has a smaller effect on blood levels of total cholesterol and harmful LDL cholesterol than does the mix of fats in the diet","entailment":"The sentence compares the impact of dietary cholesterol versus dietary fats on blood cholesterol, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"cholesterol in food","comparator":"mix of fats in the diet","outcome":"blood total and LDL cholesterol levels","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"smaller effect","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Eating one egg a day is generally safe for the heart.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"a 2008 report from the Physicians’ Health Study supports the idea that eating an egg a day is generally safe for the heart","entailment":"The quoted report explicitly states that daily egg consumption is generally heart‑safe, backing the claim.","population":"","intervention":"eating an egg a day","comparator":"","outcome":"heart safety","timeframe":"2008 report","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Consuming far more than one egg a day could increase the risk for heart failure later in life.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"it also suggests that going much beyond that could increase the risk for heart failure later in life","entailment":"The source indicates that exceeding one egg per day may raise later heart‑failure risk, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"consuming far more than one egg per day","comparator":"","outcome":"risk for heart failure later in life","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"much beyond that","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"People with diabetes who ate one or more eggs a day had increased heart disease risk.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"heart disease risk was increased among men and women with diabetes who ate one or more eggs a day","entailment":"The quoted finding directly links daily egg consumption in diabetics to higher heart disease risk.","population":"men and women with diabetes","intervention":"one or more eggs a day","comparator":"","outcome":"heart disease risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"one or more eggs a day","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"For people with diabetes and heart disease, limiting egg consumption to no more than three yolks per week may be best.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"it may be best to limit egg consumption to no more than three yolks per week","entailment":"The source recommends a maximum of three yolks weekly for diabetics with heart disease, supporting the claim.","population":"people with diabetes and heart disease","intervention":"limit egg consumption to no more than three yolks per week","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"no more than three yolks per week","modality":"normative"},{"statement":"Whole grains and fruit predict lower risk of heart disease.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Consumption of whole grains and fruit predict lower risk of heart disease","entailment":"The quoted sentence states that eating whole grains and fruit is predictive of reduced heart‑disease risk.","population":"","intervention":"consumption of whole grains and fruit","comparator":"","outcome":"risk of heart disease","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Plant sources like nuts and seeds are related to lower cardiovascular and overall mortality compared with red meat or eggs.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"plant sources like nuts and seeds are related to lower cardiovascular and overall mortality, especially when compared to red meat or eggs","entailment":"The source directly compares plant protein sources to red meat or eggs, indicating lower mortality, which supports the claim.","population":"","intervention":"plant sources like nuts and seeds","comparator":"red meat or eggs","outcome":"cardiovascular and overall mortality","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"}]}
